title: 英検2級 2026年度 第1回 リスニング

source.segmentation: ffmpeg silencedetectで約10秒の解答無音を検出し、各設問ごとのMP3クリップを生成。

lessons.question: What do we learn about the woman?
choices: She knows the waiter well.
She always orders the fish.
She has been waiting for a long time.
She goes to the restaurant quite often.
lessons.script:
☆Excuse me, sir. We've been waiting for almost an hour. How long will it be before we can get a
table?
★I'm terribly sorry, ma'am. It should only be a few more minutes.
☆Could we at least get a menu to look at while we wait? We'd like to decide what to order.
★Certainly. I'll bring you one right away. Personally, I recommend the fish of the day. It's
delicious!
☆☆
tips: 短縮形は弱く速く出ます: I'll, I'm, It's, We'd, We've

lessons.question: What does the woman say?
choices: She did not live in China.
She likes to send postcards.
She will take a vacation this year.
She cannot read Chinese.
lessons.script:
★Didn't you say that you used to live in China, Joan?
☆Yeah. I lived there for several years. Why?
★Well, my friend went there on vacation recently, and he sent me this postcard. The writing on the
front is Chinese. Can you read it?
☆Sorry, Dan. I speak Chinese pretty well, but I never learned how to read it.
☆☆

lessons.question: What do we learn about the man?
choices: He is going to his office.
He ate too much on the plane.
He just came back from a trip.
He works at a convenience store.
lessons.script:
☆Thanks for choosing Redville Airport Taxi. Where to?
★Well, I'm going to my home on Fifth Street. But if you don't mind, I'd like to stop by a
convenience store first.
☆No problem, sir. Did you have a nice flight?
★Yes. But the airline didn't serve any food. I need to get something to eat.
☆☆

There's a bank there.

lessons.question: How will the woman probably travel to the downtown area?
choices: She will take a bus.
She will take a taxi.
She will ride her bike.
She will drive her car.
lessons.script:
☆Excuse me. I'm in a hurry, and I need to get to the downtown area in about twenty minutes. Is
there a bus stop nearby?
★There's a stop about a block that way, but you should call a taxi if you're really in a hurry.
☆Oh, really? How long does the bus ride take?
★About forty-five minutes. A taxi would only take fifteen.
☆☆

Read books about old temples.
Take a vacation to visit his daughter.

lessons.script:
★Ms. Robbins, I'd like to ask for a week of vacation in June.
☆Sure, David. You haven't taken a vacation in a long time. You deserve a break.
★Thanks. I'm going to visit my daughter in Kyoto. She started going to a university there this year.
☆That's great! Kyoto has some of the most amazing temples I've ever seen.
☆☆

★Hi, honey. I'm missing a folder that had some important documents in it. Have you seen it?

★Thank goodness. I was worried I had left it on the train. Could you possibly bring it to me at my
office? I need it for a meeting this afternoon.
☆Sure. I'm going downtown for lunch anyway. I'll take it to you then.

lessons.question: Why did the man call the woman?
choices: To tell her about a new restaurant.
To ask about what to do on her birthday.
To suggest that she make a reservation.
To ask where she ate dinner. Listening Test
lessons.script:
☆Hello.
★Hi, Betty! It's Martin. I'm calling to ask if you've decided what you want to do for your birthday.
☆Actually, I haven't really thought about it. Hmm. What about eating at that fancy new Thai
restaurant that opened downtown?
★Great! Can you tell me the name of the place? I'll make a reservation.
☆It's called Diva's Dish. It's on Beach Avenue.
☆☆

lessons.script:
☆Hello. Marigold Theater. How can I help you?
★Hello. I have tickets to the play tonight. Can you tell me how to get to the theater?
☆Sure. It's on the corner of Fifth Street and Parker Avenue. You can get there by city bus, but there
may not be space to park if you come by car.
★Oh, great! I can take the bus, then. Thanks a lot.
☆☆

He does not like Kyoto.
The trains were late today. Listening Test 第 2部
lessons.script:
☆Jun, how do you like living in Kyoto?
★I really like it, but I have trouble getting to work on time. The trains are always so full in the
morning.
☆I'm sorry to hear that. Were they running late today, too?
★No, they were actually on time. I just couldn't get on one of the first trains this morning.
☆☆

The airport is too busy.
There is a problem with the airplane.

lessons.script:
★Attention, passengers. This is an announcement for all passengers on Flight 290 to Denver. We
are sorry to inform you that your flight will be delayed because of bad weather. We expect to leave
in about two hours. We apologize for the delay. If you would like to cancel your flight, please come
to the ticket counter, and we will help you find another flight.
☆☆

choices: He made a good presentation.
He wrote an article about the solar system.
He studied the history of planets.
He named the moons of planets.
lessons.script:

presentation about anything related to science. Daniel made a model of the solar system and
explained how the planets move around the sun. His presentation was so good that he won first
prize. Daniel was very happy, and he is already thinking about next year's science fair.
☆☆

lessons.question: What do many hotels offer to their guests?
choices: Exercise classes to relax after dinner.
Healthy dining options by beauty experts.
Fun entertainment and activities during the day.
Good beds and goods for better sleep.
lessons.script:
★More and more people are suffering from a lack of sleep these days. When planning vacations,
some people place more importance on sleep than on sightseeing and other activities. As a result,
the term "sleep tourism" has become popular recently. Many hotels provide their guests with
comfortable beds and products for good sleep. Some hotels even offer sleep experts' treatments to
their guests.
☆☆

Looking for a new job.
Opening an art school.
Painting pictures as a hobby.
lessons.script:
☆Sarah is an accountant, and she works long hours every day. Last year, she started painting as a
hobby. She wanted to find a way to relax and express her creativity. Sarah took a painting class and
found that she really enjoyed painting landscapes. Now, she spends time outdoors on weekends to
find inspiration. She plans to have an exhibition of her paintings next spring.
☆☆
tips: 数字・時制表現は選択肢の根拠になりやすいので、単語だけでなく周辺表現も確認する。
第2部は1文目で場面、最後の文で結論が出やすい。冒頭と末尾を特に丁寧に聞く。
Question の疑問詞を確認し、何を答える問題かを先に固定する。

lessons.question: Why did James join the drama club?
choices: He wanted to improve his acting skills.
He wanted to meet new students.
His best friend was a member.
His teacher told him to.
lessons.script:
★James is a high school student who loves acting. He used to practice acting by himself at home,
but he wanted to get better at it, so he joined his school's drama club. Last month, the club
performed a play, and James was one of the actors. He had a great time working with the other
members of the club, and he is looking forward to the next performance.
☆☆
tips: 短縮形は弱く速く出ます: school's
used to は語の切れ目より、ひとまとまりの音で拾う。
数字・時制表現は選択肢の根拠になりやすいので、単語だけでなく周辺表現も確認する。
第2部は1文目で場面、最後の文で結論が出やすい。冒頭と末尾を特に丁寧に聞く。
Question の疑問詞を確認し、何を答える問題かを先に固定する。

lessons.question: What did David do last weekend?
choices: He attended a Korean language lesson.
He decided to learn the Korean language.
He went to sell some books about Korea.
He met some new friends from Korea.
lessons.script:
☆David wanted to learn a new language but was not sure which one. So, last weekend, he went to
the language section of a bookstore. He had an idea. He would learn the language of the first book
he picked without looking. He closed his eyes and reached for a book. It was a Korean language
book. David chose two other related books and was ready to learn Korean.
☆☆
tips: 数字・時制表現は選択肢の根拠になりやすいので、単語だけでなく周辺表現も確認する。
第2部は1文目で場面、最後の文で結論が出やすい。冒頭と末尾を特に丁寧に聞く。
Question の疑問詞を確認し、何を答える問題かを先に固定する。

lessons.question: What is David's plan?
choices: To find a job near his house.
To help his mother cook dinner.
To eat dinner at a restaurant.
To stop working parttime at night.
lessons.script:
★David is a university student, and he wants to spend more time with his family. He enjoys eating
his mother's delicious dinners, but his mother is often busy with work during the week. David had
an idea. He will help his mother cook dinner. That way, he can spend more time with his family, and
he can also learn how to cook.
☆☆
tips: 短縮形は弱く速く出ます: mother's
場所・施設名は内容理解の柱です。前置詞とセットで聞き取る。
数字・時制表現は選択肢の根拠になりやすいので、単語だけでなく周辺表現も確認する。
第2部は1文目で場面、最後の文で結論が出やすい。冒頭と末尾を特に丁寧に聞く。
